title: Amazon Commercial Services Pty Ltd — Consumer harm
investigation_objective: The ACCC commenced Federal Court proceedings on 29 June 2026 alleging Amazon Commercial Services Pty Ltd included five unfair terms in its standard-form Prime subscription contracts, held by more than one million annual subscribers between November 2023 and August 2025, letting it unilaterally degrade services or alter conditions without pro-rata refunds. The ACCC further alleges Amazon relied on these terms in July 2024 to introduce advertising into Prime Video without compensating subscribers, who then had to pay an extra $2.99 per month to remain ad-free. This is among the first contested cases under Australia's unfair contract terms civil penalty regime.
scope: Subject of Federal Court proceedings brought by the ACCC alleging unfair contract terms, ongoing; Alleged to have relied on contested contract terms to introduce advertising without compensating over one million subscribers; Among the first contested cases under Australia's unfair contract terms civil penalty regime
